content.text:
You are an expert cardiac electrophysiology and quantitative pharmacology assistant.

Your task is to write ONLY the LaTeX text for the Results section of a manuscript, describing simulation results for quinidine.

In THIS response, you must ONLY describe the Results subsections corresponding to Figures 13–16. Do NOT mention any figures prior to Figure 13 and beyond Figure 16. Every Figures need to be explained in one paragraph.

Structure:
- Write 4 comprehensive paragraphs, one for each of Figures 13–16, in order.
- Describe what the feature is, explain the boxplot elements and units, describe at least one trend across concentrations, give at least one concrete numeric example from the feature summary CSV (median or bounds), and interpret what the pattern means for TdP risk or electrophysiological behavior.

Fixed meanings:
- Figure 13: distribution of qNet values across concentrations (boxplot), with the LaTex label {fig:features-qnet}.
- Figure 14: distribution of qInward values across concentrations (boxplot), with the LaTex label {fig:features-qinward}.
- Figure 15: distribution of APD90 values across concentrations (boxplot), with the LaTex label {fig:features-apd90}.
- Figure 16: distribution of APD50 values across concentrations (boxplot), with the LaTex label {fig:features-apd50}.

STRICT LATEX RULES:
- Output MUST be valid LaTeX body content only (no \documentclass, no preamble, no \begin{document}).
- Do not make any new section or subsection.
- Do NOT use Markdown at all: no backticks, no ``` fences, no # headings, no `*` or `**` for emphasis.
- Do NOT use any LaTeX SI-unit helper commands (such as those from the siunitx package). Write all units explicitly as plain text (e.g., ms, nA/uF, nM) next to the numbers, either in text or math mode.
- Use standard LaTeX sectioning commands such as \section, \subsection, \paragraph as needed.
- You may use math mode with $...$ or \( ... \), but do NOT wrap the entire answer in a single math environment.
- Use ASCII characters only; avoid Unicode symbols.
- Make sure all braces { } and math modes are balanced.

Please generate the LaTeX text for the Results subsections corresponding to Figures 13–16 only, using the attached CSV time-series data, features summary, and LaTeX template as guidance. Follow the style and level of detail implied by the template.
